summary: Large symbol-table/config update that imported many names and metadata from build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map into config/GALE01/symbols.txt, replacing many address-only fn_XXXXXXXX placeholders with map-derived names and adding explicit type, size, and scope fields. The PR also added a Rust workspace tool, tools/config-dtk, intended to parse the linker map with cwparse and emit symbols.txt, plus minor workspace/config plumbing.
what_changed: Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt with 11227 added and 10605 deleted lines, a broad regeneration/import rather than isolated manual renames.
Replaced many generic symbols such as fn_80005940 with map-derived names such as Command_00, Command_Execute, lbColl_80005BB0, lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix, and TRK_memcpy where GALE01.map provided names.

Normalized/expanded symbol annotations in symbols.txt, including type:function/object/label, size:0x..., and scope:global/local/weak fields; examples include changing __check_pad3 and __init_data to scope:global based on map data.
Added the new Rust crate tools/config-dtk named melee-config-dtk and added it to the top-level Cargo workspace members.

tools/config-dtk parses build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map, walks source paths, collects tree symbols and table symbols, joins them by a hash of translation unit plus identifier, sorts output by address with BTreeMap, and writes config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
tools/config-dtk maps linker-map section names to config section strings including .text, .data, .rodata, .bss, .sdata, .sdata2, .sbss, .sbss2, .init, .ctors, .dtors, extab, and extabindex.
configure.py progress mode now sets config.progress_all = False before calculate_progress(config).
Added placeholder files orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep and tools/__init__.py.
The PR included tools/config-dtk/.idea project files and a standalone LICENSE for the new tool.
smart_moves: Used the official/generated linker map as a bulk source of symbol names and metadata instead of hand-renaming thousands of addresses.
Kept symbol output deterministic by sorting generated symbol strings by address before writing symbols.txt.
Joined two complementary map views: the tree view supplies name/type/scope and the section table supplies section/address/size/alignment.
Keyed symbols by translation unit plus identifier, reducing accidental collisions between repeated local or instance-qualified map names.
Used cwparse to parse CodeWarrior map syntax rather than ad-hoc string splitting.
Handled section-name conversion explicitly, which makes emitted config section names consistent with the existing symbols.txt format.
Preserved the existing address-based fallback convention where a better map-derived name was not available; not every fn_XXXXXXXX was replaced.
Filtered source-path collection to .c files and stripped .o suffixes from translation-unit names, matching the C-source-oriented decomp workflow.
decomp_lessons: For large naming passes, generate symbols.txt from GALE01.map and keep type, size, scope, and section metadata together; this is safer and more reviewable than piecemeal manual edits.
When importing linker-map names, preserve deterministic ordering by address so diffs remain stable across regenerations.
Map-derived names can immediately improve searchability and decomp context even before functions are fully decompiled, especially for library-like areas such as TRK and lbColl.
Do not assume all map entries are equally useful: this PR title says "most symbols," and the tool code explicitly ignores some entries, including .s.o translation units and debug/child cases.
Section handling should be explicit; extab/extabindex and small-data sections need correct config names just like .text/.data/.rodata.
Bulk symbol imports can affect progress tooling and lockfiles; include the generator/tooling context so future maintainers can reproduce or audit the generated symbols.
Evidence for reviewer intent is weak here because the dump contains no PR body, issue comments, review comments, or review records.
naming_conventions: Address-only unknown function placeholders use fn_XXXXXXXX, e.g. fn_800051CC; these remain when no better imported name is available.
Map-derived global names are preferred over fn_XXXXXXXX when available, e.g. TRK_memcpy, Command_00, Command_Execute, lbColl_80005BB0, lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix.
Generated symbol lines follow: name = section:0xADDRESS; // type:<function|object|label> size:0xSIZE scope:<global|local|weak>.
config-dtk formats instance-qualified named identifiers as name$instance.
config-dtk formats DotL linker labels as lbl<name>.
config-dtk formats section identifiers without an index as the section string and section identifiers with an index as ..<section>.<idx>.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR was primarily symbol/config generation, not a C matching change.
The tool reads build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map via a memory map and parses lines in parallel with rayon par_lines.
It only records tree and section-table symbols from translation units ending in .c.o, while .s.o entries are ignored, so imported names are oriented toward C-object decomp output.
It treats tree::Type::None as label, tree::Type::Object as object, and tree::Type::Function as function for symbols.txt output.
It uses memory-table entries to collect section records and section-symbol entries to infer split starts/ends, although split writing appears unfinished or unused in the diff excerpt.
It warns on zero-size table symbols and child symbols rather than silently treating them as normal parent entries.
It aborts on duplicate insertion for the same translation-unit/identifier key, a useful safeguard for generated config correctness.
review_feedback: No human review comments, issue comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the dump, so no explicit reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify or remove tools/config-dtk or its generated output.
Find later comments or commits explaining why tools/config-dtk included .idea files and a standalone Cargo.lock.
Search for subsequent symbols.txt regenerations from GALE01.map to see whether this became the standard workflow.
Audit whether config-dtk's .c.o-only filtering missed useful assembly object symbols.
Check later configure.py changes around config.progress_all to understand the progress-mode impact.
